module_info.docstring:
Shared auxiliary OpenAI client for cheap/fast side tasks.

Provides a single resolution chain so every consumer (context compression,
session search, web extraction, vision analysis, browser vision) picks up
the best available backend without duplicating fallback logic.

Resolution order for text tasks:
  1. OpenRouter  (OPENROUTER_API_KEY)
  2. Nous Portal (~/.hermes/auth.json active provider)
  3. Custom endpoint (OPENAI_BASE_URL + OPENAI_API_KEY)
  4. Codex OAuth (Responses API via chatgpt.com with gpt-5.3-codex,
     wrapped to look like a chat.completions client)
  5. None

Resolution order for vision/multimodal tasks:
  1. OpenRouter
  2. Nous Portal
  3. None  (custom endpoints can't substitute for Gemini multimodal)

classes.name: _CodexCompletionsAdapter
classes.docstring:
Drop-in shim that accepts chat.completions.create() kwargs and
routes them through the Codex Responses streaming API.

classes.name: _CodexChatShim
classes.docstring: Wraps the adapter to provide client.chat.completions.create().

classes.name: CodexAuxiliaryClient
classes.docstring:
OpenAI-client-compatible wrapper that routes through Codex Responses API.

Consumers can call client.chat.completions.create(**kwargs) as normal.
Also exposes .api_key and .base_url for introspection by async wrappers.

classes.name: _AsyncCodexCompletionsAdapter
classes.docstring:
Async version of the Codex Responses adapter.

Wraps the sync adapter via asyncio.to_thread() so async consumers
(web_tools, session_search) can await it as normal.

functions.name: _read_nous_auth
functions.docstring:
Read and validate ~/.hermes/auth.json for an active Nous provider.

Returns the provider state dict if Nous is active with tokens,
otherwise None.

functions.name: _nous_api_key
functions.docstring: Extract the best API key from a Nous provider state dict.

functions.name: _nous_base_url
functions.docstring: Resolve the Nous inference base URL from env or default.

functions.name: _read_codex_access_token
functions.docstring: Read a valid Codex OAuth access token from Hermes auth store (~/.hermes/auth.json).

functions.name: get_text_auxiliary_client
functions.docstring:
Return (client, model_slug) for text-only auxiliary tasks.

Falls through OpenRouter -> Nous Portal -> custom endpoint -> Codex OAuth -> (None, None).

functions.name: get_async_text_auxiliary_client
functions.docstring:
Return (async_client, model_slug) for async consumers.

For standard providers returns (AsyncOpenAI, model). For Codex returns
(AsyncCodexAuxiliaryClient, model) which wraps the Responses API.
Returns (None, None) when no provider is available.

functions.name: get_vision_auxiliary_client
functions.docstring:
Return (client, model_slug) for vision/multimodal auxiliary tasks.

Only OpenRouter and Nous Portal qualify — custom endpoints cannot
substitute for Gemini multimodal.

functions.name: get_auxiliary_extra_body
functions.docstring:
Return extra_body kwargs for auxiliary API calls.

Includes Nous Portal product tags when the auxiliary client is backed
by Nous Portal. Returns empty dict otherwise.

functions.name: auxiliary_max_tokens_param
functions.docstring:
Return the correct max tokens kwarg for the auxiliary client's provider.

OpenRouter and local models use 'max_tokens'. Direct OpenAI with newer
models (gpt-4o, o-series, gpt-5+) requires 'max_completion_tokens'.
The Codex adapter translates max_tokens internally, so we use max_tokens
for it as well.
